---
title: "Source Guide: Matrices and Linear Maps"
description: "A curated source guide for learning matrices as linear maps from first pass through research bridge."
page-type: source-guide
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/concepts/matrices-and-linear-maps.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose the right resource for the operator viewpoint at different depths."
  - "Separate durable matrix references from current architecture-facing bridges."
  - "See which sources belong to the concept, application, and research pages in this package."
applications:
  - machine-learning
  - control
keywords:
  - source guide
  - matrices
  - linear maps
  - operators
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "MIT 18.06SC Linear Transformations and their Matrices"
  - "Stanford VMLS"
  - "Hefferon, Linear Algebra"
current-sources:
  - "Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective (2025)"
  - "Attention Is All You Need"
---

## How To Use This Guide

Use one source per layer:

- `First pass` for the operator viewpoint
- `Second pass` for extra exercises and coordinate discipline
- `Paper bridge` for seeing matrices inside modern systems

## Stable Core

- [MIT 18.06SC: Linear Transformations and their Matrices](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/pages/positive-definite-matrices-and-applications/linear-transformations-and-their-matrices/) - `First pass` - best official source for the core theorem that columns are basis images. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Introduction to Applied Linear Algebra – Vectors, Matrices, and Least Squares](https://web.stanford.edu/~boyd/vmls/) - `First pass` - good applied treatment of transformations and matrix computation. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - stronger proof and exercise depth for linear maps and coordinate representations. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Current Bridge

- [Deep learning, transformers and graph neural networks: a linear algebra perspective](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11075-025-02218-2) - `Second pass` - current survey showing how operator language remains useful in AI systems. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Paper Bridge

- [Attention is All you Need](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2017/hash/3f5ee243547dee91fbd053c1c4a845aa-Abstract.html) - `Paper bridge` - canonical architecture paper where learned projections are explicit matrix maps. Checked `2026-04-24`.

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Path 1: First serious understanding

1. MIT 18.06SC Linear Transformations and their Matrices
2. [Matrices and Linear Maps](../concepts/matrices-and-linear-maps.qmd)
3. Hefferon for extra exercises

### Path 2: Operator intuition with visuals

1. [Matrices and Linear Maps](../concepts/matrices-and-linear-maps.qmd)
2. [Computation Lab: Matrix Composition and Basis Action](../labs/matrix-composition-and-basis-action.qmd)
3. [Exercises: Matrices and Linear Maps](../exercises/matrices-and-linear-maps.qmd)

### Path 3: Bridge to modern ML systems

1. [Matrices and Linear Maps](../concepts/matrices-and-linear-maps.qmd)
2. [Learned Linear Projections in Transformers](../applications/learned-linear-projections-in-transformers.qmd)
3. [Linear Operators in Modern AI Systems](../paper-lab/linear-operators-in-modern-ai-systems.qmd)

## Sources Checked Online

All links above were checked on `2026-04-24`.

The verification pass included:

- official MIT OCW pages
- official Stanford or author-maintained textbook pages
- official NeurIPS proceedings page
- current journal page for the 2025 linear-algebra perspective survey
